headlines.title: Two Britons self-isolating in UK after leaving hantavirus cruise ship
headlines.description: A British man is among three people evacuated from the ship while two other Britons are self-isolating in the UK.

headlines.title: Iran considering US proposal as Trump says war will be 'over quickly'
headlines.description: Mediator Pakistan says it is "endeavouring to convert this ceasefire into a permanent end to this war".

headlines.title: The AI fitness instructors selling unreal gains
headlines.description: A BBC Sport investigation finds misleading adverts for fitness apps that use AI-generated instructors to make exaggerated claims.

headlines.title: Watch: Drone delivers first Amazon parcels in UK
headlines.description: The tech giant says it hopes to slowly expand the service as the demand for ultra-fast deliveries grows.

headlines.title: Serving Met officer and four ex-officers investigated for handling of Al Fayed allegations
headlines.description: A serving officer and four former officers are being investigated over the handling of reports against the businessman.

headlines.title: Woman covertly filmed for 'humiliating' social media content - then told to pay
headlines.description: Alice says the video was posted on social media and viewed about 40,000 times.

headlines.title: German tourist wins payout after losing sun lounger race
headlines.description: The man sued his tour operator for allowing people to reserve sun loungers with towels despite a hotel ban on the practice.

headlines.title: Shell latest oil giant to see profits surge due to Iran war impact
headlines.description: The energy giant's profits jump by nearly a quarter as it benefits from the recent volatility in the oil price

headlines.title: Fifa president offers $2m World Cup ticket buyers 'a hot dog and a Coke'
headlines.description: Fifa president Gianni Infantino says he will "personally bring a hot dog and a Coke" to anyone who buys a World Cup final ticket for $2m.

headlines.title: What you need to know about the 7 May elections in England, Scotland and Wales
headlines.description: Millions will vote in parliamentary elections in Scotland and Wales, and local elections in England.

headlines.title: Do I need a photo ID and my polling card to vote in the elections?
headlines.description: Voters in England only will have to show photo ID while polling cards are not essential.

headlines.title: Who can I vote for in my area?
headlines.description: Elections are taking place across the UK. Find out whether there is one where you live and who you can vote for.

headlines.title: How does the BBC report polling day?
headlines.description: The BBC cannot report on campaigning or election issues while polls are open on Thursday 7 May.

headlines.title: How cameras are being used to tackle abuse in nurseries
headlines.description: With reports of abuse and deaths fuelling concern among parents, questions are growing over whether CCTV can offer real protection for children - or not.

headlines.title: Ukraine is a global surrogacy hub - but that could be about to end
headlines.description: Ukraine is considering a law that would effectively ban foreigners from accessing surrogacy in the country.

headlines.title: Satellite images reveal key bridge linking North Korea and Russia is almost complete
headlines.description: The road crossing is the latest sign of the deepening relationship between Pyongyang and Moscow.

headlines.title: The no-nonsense judge calling the shots in Musk v Altman trial
headlines.description: The feud has fuelled a costly showdown between two tech titans.

headlines.title: When Scotland qualified for the World Cup... but didn't go
headlines.description: Scotland waited 28 years to be part of this year's World Cup - back in 1950 they turned it down.

headlines.title: 'Enjoy the show. Ignore the war': Venice Biennale faces backlash after including Russia
headlines.description: Protests have taken place at the prestigious arts fair over the inclusion of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

headlines.title: How loan shark threats keep victims like me silent
headlines.description: A meat cleaver and samurai sword are among items seized from suspects, shown exclusively to the BBC.

headlines.title: Looksmaxxing influencer Clavicular charged over alleged alligator shooting
headlines.description: The looksmaxxing influencer allegedly livestreamed himself firing gunshots from an airboat at an alligator.

headlines.title: Watch: Passengers told virus-hit ship 'not infectious' after first death
headlines.description: YouTuber Ruhi Çenet was on board the ship, and filmed the announcement made on 12 April.

headlines.title: Islamic State-linked women arrive home in Australia from Syria
headlines.description: A group of nine women and children has landed in Melbourne while another woman and her child arrived in Sydney.

headlines.title: Harry Potter TV series renewed for second season at HBO
headlines.description: Filming is scheduled for autumn later this year and will likely adapt the second book in JK Rowling's series, The Chamber of Secrets.

headlines.title: Traveller site construction 'in breach of law'
headlines.description: A council orders all work on the land, which began on bank holiday weekend, to cease immediately.

headlines.title: Driver arrested after chasing down child cyclist on footpath
headlines.description: Video captures young boy on bike fleeing car that mounts footpath in Washington State

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Electioncast: It’s Time To Vote!
headlines.description: Adam, Laura, Chris, James and Felicity bring you one last update before the polls open.

headlines.title: Is Donald Trump close to finishing his war with Iran?
headlines.description: Or has the US president lost control?

headlines.title: From ego-ridden team to complete package - why PSG pose ultimate test
headlines.description: Luis Enrique's expertise at rebuilding a culture as well as a team means Arsenal will confront the complete package when they meet holders PSG in the Champions League final.

headlines.title: Liverpool reduce planned rise in ticket prices
headlines.description: Liverpool cut the size of their planned rise in ticket prices for the next couple of seasons after fan protests.

headlines.title: Did PSG 'get away' with handball decisions?
headlines.description: BBC Sport pundits Nedum Onuoha, Guillem Balague and Stephen Warnock analyse two contentious handball decisions that weren't awarded against Paris St-Germain, including a potential red card for Nuno Mendes and a possible penalty for Bayern Munich.

headlines.title: Is 15-year-old IPL wonderkid ready to play for India?
headlines.description: What makes Indian sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi so effective and what could be next for the 15-year-old after his Indian Premier League success?

headlines.title: Villa's Staniforth 'at ease' with decision to retire
headlines.description: Aston Villa midfielder and former England international Lucy Staniforth did not hesitate when she made the decision to retire from football at the end of the season.

headlines.title: Infantino's hot dog & soft drink pledge over $2m ticket
headlines.description: Fifa president Gianni Infantino says he will "personally bring a hot dog and a Coke" to anyone who buys a World Cup final ticket for $2m.
